name: Maxwell-Boltzmann speed distribution
principles: equilibrium statistical mechanics of a classical ideal gas
factorisation of the Boltzmann distribution exp(-E / k_B T) over independent translational degrees of freedom
isotropy of momentum space — distribution depends only on |v|, not direction
formulaTeX: f(v)\, dv \;=\; 4\pi\, n \left(\frac{m}{2\pi k_B T}\right)^{3/2} v^2 \exp\!\left(-\,\frac{m\, v^2}{2 k_B T}\right) dv
conventions: f(v) dv is the number density of particles with speeds in [v, v + dv]; n is the total number density
m is the molecular mass; k_B = 1.380649e-23 J/K is the Boltzmann constant
T is the absolute temperature (K) — only valid in thermal equilibrium
the v^2 prefactor comes from the surface area of a sphere of radius v in velocity space (4 pi v^2 dv)
valid for a classical ideal gas: dilute (de Broglie wavelength much smaller than interparticle spacing) and non-degenerate (k_B T >> Fermi energy / level spacing)
non-relativistic: requires v << c; for relativistic gases use Maxwell-Jüttner distribution
expectedLimits: most probable speed (mode of f(v)): v_p = sqrt(2 k_B T / m)
mean speed: <v> = sqrt(8 k_B T / (pi m)) = (2 / sqrt(pi)) v_p
rms speed: v_rms = sqrt(3 k_B T / m) = sqrt(3/2) v_p — connects directly to mean kinetic energy <(1/2) m v^2> = (3/2) k_B T (equipartition)

low-T limit T -> 0: distribution collapses toward a delta at v = 0; classical picture must be replaced by Bose-Einstein or Fermi-Dirac statistics once de Broglie wavelength becomes comparable to interparticle spacing
high-T limit: distribution broadens, fraction of particles above any fixed escape speed grows monotonically — drives Jeans escape from planetary atmospheres
Knudsen regime (mean free path comparable to system size): the bulk distribution holds but boundary effects dominate transport
specific cases: N2 at 300 K -> v_p ≈ 422 m/s, <v> ≈ 476 m/s, v_rms ≈ 517 m/s; H2 at 300 K -> v_p ≈ 1573 m/s
consistency check: <v^2> = 3 k_B T / m must agree with kinetic theory pressure p = (1/3) n m <v^2> = n k_B T (ideal gas law)
references: Maxwell (1860) — Illustrations of the Dynamical Theory of Gases, Phil. Mag.
Boltzmann (1872) — Weitere Studien über das Wärmegleichgewicht unter Gasmolekülen
Reif — Fundamentals of Statistical and Thermal Physics, ch.7
BIPM — SI Brochure (defining value of k_B)
